Do newly hatched larvae sneak in small enough to avoid detection, then gradually acquire the colony scent through contact? How does a fly evolve breathing holes that look like termite eyes? Nobody knows yet.

The study, published in Current Biology in early 2025, adds to a growing body of evidence that social insect colonies — long considered fortresses of cooperative defense — are riddled with freeloaders who've had millions of years of evolutionary pressure to crack the gates. The most striking implication is just how hackable these systems are. Termite colonies coordinate millions of individuals using a relatively simple chemical language, and that simplicity, while brilliantly efficient for cooperation, is precisely what makes it vulnerable to exploitation by anything patient enough to learn the password.

Sources:

  • https://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822(25)00007-7
  • https://www.sciencenews.org/article/butt-blowfly-larvae-mimic-termite-faces
  • https://ellipse.prbb.org/the-smiling-fly-who-wanted-to-be-a-termite/
  • https://www.livescience.com/animals/insects/moroccan-fly-maggot-uses-fake-face-on-its-butt-to-infiltrate-termite-colony

item.title: Meta 发布 MTIA 四代芯片路线图：每六个月一代，推理性能号称超越 NVIDIA
item.description:
3 月 11 日，Meta 公布了自研 AI 芯片 MTIA（Meta Training and Inference Accelerator）的四代产品路线图：MTIA 300、400、450、500，计划在两年内全部部署。这个节奏是行业平均水平的两倍——芯片厂商通常 12-18 个月才能迭代一代，Meta 要做到六个月一颗。

四代芯片均由 Broadcom 合作开发，定位清晰：MTIA 300 已经在生产环境中跑推荐系统训练；MTIA 400 实验室测试完毕，即将进数据中心，FP8 算力比 300 高 400%，HBM 带宽高 51%，官方说法是"原始性能与市面顶级商用产品竞争力相当"——这里的"顶级商用产品"指的显然是 NVIDIA 的 GPU。到了 MTIA 450（2027 年初量产），措辞变成了"远超现有顶级商用产品"，HBM 带宽再翻倍，MX4 算力比 FP16 高 6 倍。MTIA 500 在 450 基础上再加 50% HBM 带宽和 80% 容量，同样 2027 年量产。从 300 到 500，HBM 带宽提升 4.5 倍，算力提升 25 倍。

技术路线上有几个值得关注的选择。首先是"推理优先"——Meta 明确说主流 GPU 为大规模预训练优化，用来做推理成本效率不够高。MTIA 450/500 专门为生成式 AI 推理设计，支持 MX8 和 MX4 低精度格式，内建 attention 和 FFN 硬件加速。其次是软件栈选择：原生支持 PyTorch、vLLM、Triton，编译器基于 TorchInductor 和 MLIR——这意味着 Meta 内部的模型工程师切换芯片的迁移成本很低。第三，芯片采用模块化 chiplet 架构，MTIA 400 是双计算 chiplet，MTIA 500 是 2x2 四 chiplet 配置加独立 SoC chiplet 处理 PCIe 连接。

这对 Meta 的 AI 基础设施版图意味着什么？Meta 目前的策略是"组合拳"：NVIDIA GPU 做预训练，MTIA 做推理和推荐。考虑到 Meta 的 AI 产品——Meta AI 助手、广告推荐、内容排序——每天要服务数十亿用户，推理侧的算力消耗远大于训练侧。自研推理芯片如果真能做到性价比超过 NVIDIA，省下的钱是天文数字。Broadcom 已经表态 Meta 将在 2027 年部署"数个吉瓦"的 MTIA 芯片。结合之前通过 Nebius 锁定的 270 亿美元 Vera Rubin 算力合同，Meta 正在同时押注 NVIDIA 训练端和自研推理端，两条腿走路。

当然，路线图画得漂亮不等于执行到位。HBM 全行业短缺是公认难题，Meta 自己也承认供应链是风险。MTIA 400 的"与顶级产品竞争"到 450 的"远超"之间，跨度是否过于乐观？等 2027 年实际部署数据出来才能下结论。

Sources:

  • https://ai.meta.com/blog/meta-mtia-scale-ai-chips-for-billions/
  • https://about.fb.com/news/2026/03/expanding-metas-custom-silicon-to-power-our-ai-workloads/
  • https://www.theregister.com/2026/03/12/meta_custom_chips/
  • https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/semiconductors/meta-reveals-four-new-mtia-chips-built-for-ai-inference

item.title: 密歇根女篮 92-63 大胜 NC State 晋级 Sweet 16，男女篮同时挺进第二周末
item.description:
昨天下午的 Crisler Center，密歇根女篮用一场 92-63 的碾压式胜利送走了 NC State，拿到了队史第三次 Sweet 16 门票。

比赛开局其实挺揪心——首节密歇根投篮命中率只有 33%，三分线外 0-for-5，一度 12-13 落后。转折点在第二节：大二后卫 Syla Swords 连续突破得手后接一记深三分，点燃了一波 14-0 的攻击潮。不过 NC State 半场前又回敬了一波 12-2，半场只领先 3 分（28-25），悬念还在。

下半场完全是密歇根的表演。第三节球队打出 32 分，逼出对手 10 次失误、7 次抢断。Olivia Olson 在下半场独砍 27 分——对，下半场全部 27 分——包括 7 记三分球。Swords 全场贡献 26 分，Mila Holloway 拿下 6 次助攻。第四节比赛早早进入垃圾时间，替补 Alyssa Crockett 在她 Crisler Center 的最后一场主场比赛中获得了充足上场时间。

NC State 缺少了因伤缺阵的主力控卫 Zoe Brooks（此前对阵田纳西时受伤），面对密歇根的高位紧逼防守全场被迫失误 22 次。主教练 Wes Moore 赛后直言："抢断得分、内线得分、二次进攻——我们被全面压制了。"

密歇根女篮（27-6）将前往得克萨斯州 Fort Worth，在区域半决赛中迎战路易维尔与阿拉巴马的胜者。这支球队已经连续八年打进 NCAA 锦标赛，现在离队史最佳战绩只差一场球，离首次 Final Four 只差两场。

男篮这边，Sweet 16 的对手已经确定：阿拉巴马今天以 90-65 大胜德州理工，三分球 19 中，Latrell Wrightsell Jr. 砍下 24 分。这意味着 1 号种子密歇根（33-3）将在 3 月 27 日周五晚间于芝加哥联合中心迎战 4 号种子阿拉巴马。阿拉巴马正在创造校史纪录——连续第四年闯入 Sweet 16，实力不容小觑。

一句话：安娜堡这周末可以骄傲一下，男女篮同时进入 NCAA 锦标赛第二周末，这种好事不是年年都有。

Sources:

  • https://www.michigandaily.com/sports/womens-basketball/pushing-the-pace-no-2-seed-michigan-punches-ticket-to-sweet-sixteen-with-92-63-win-over-no-7-seed-nc-state/
  • https://247sports.com/article/michigan-basketball-dominates-nc-state-to-reach-sweet-sixteen-for-third-time-in-program-history-278613687/
  • https://rolltide.com/news/2026/3/23/mens-basketball-alabama-rolls-past-texas-tech-in-ncaa-tournament-second-round-90-65
  • https://mgoblue.com/news/2026/3/21/womens-basketball-wolverines-to-welcome-nc-state-in-ncaa-second-round

item.title: 54 岁前高管起诉 Meta 年龄歧视：50 岁以上员工被裁概率是年轻人的 2.5 倍
item.description:
3 月 20 日，Meta 前资深总监 Nicolas Franchet 在旧金山高等法院起诉 Meta，指控公司在 2025 年初的"绩效裁员"中系统性地针对年龄较大的员工。Franchet 54 岁，在 Meta 工作了近 13 年，2023 年还收到过 Zuckerberg 签名的特别股权奖励信。

诉状中引用了 Meta 向被裁员工提供的内部数据，数字相当刺眼：50 岁以上员工被裁的概率是 40 岁以下员工的 2.5 倍，40 岁以上则是 1.5 倍。2025 年 1 月 Zuckerberg 宣布砍掉 5% 的员工，公司同时上线了一套新评级系统，允许经理将员工标记为"最低绩效者"（lowest performers）。Franchet 就是被贴上这个标签后，于 2 月 10 日被解雇。他因此损失了 16,353 股未归属的 Meta 限制性股票，被裁当天价值约 1200 万美元。

这个诉讼出现的时机耐人寻味。路透社 3 月 14 日刚报道 Meta 正在酝酿更大规模的裁员——可能涉及 20%（约 15,800 人）的员工，目的是为 1350 亿美元的 AI 基础设施支出腾出预算。如果 Franchet 的指控属实，也就是说 Meta 的"绩效裁员"实际上是用算法化的评级系统包装的年龄清洗，那么即将到来的大规模裁员将面临更大的法律审查风险。

Meta 拒绝评论此案，也没有反驳诉状中引用的年龄分布数据。这种沉默本身就值得注意——如果数据有误，否认很容易。硅谷科技公司的"绩效改进计划"（PIP）和"低绩效者"标签早已被广泛批评为变相裁员的工具，但很少有人像 Franchet 这样用 Meta 自己提供的统计数据来证明歧视模式。

从更大的图景看，Meta 正在经历一场从元宇宙到 AI 的资源大转移：Reality Labs 预算砍 30%，MSL 重组裁了 600 人，Maher Saba 从 VR 部门调去搭建 AI 工程团队。这个过程中，年资长、薪酬高的老员工是最容易被当作"成本优化对象"的群体。如果法庭最终认定 Meta 的裁员存在年龄歧视模式，可能会为其他被裁的资深员工打开集体诉讼的大门。

Sources:

  • https://www.mercurynews.com/2026/03/20/meta-older-workers-layoffs-lawsuit/
  • https://www.hrkatha.com/news/meta-faces-lawsuit-alleging-age-bias-in-layoffs-senior-leader-claims-losses/
  • https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/14/meta-reportedly-considering-layoffs-that-could-affect-20-of-the-company/

item.title: Physicists Measured Negative Time — and No, It's Not Time Travel
item.description:
In a basement laboratory at the University of Toronto, physicist Aephraim Steinberg and graduate student Daniela Angulo spent two years calibrating an apparatus to measure something most people would assume is impossible: a negative duration. Not a theoretical curiosity, not a mathematical artifact — an actual, measured, negative amount of time that photons spent exciting atoms. Their result: photons passing through a cloud of ultracold rubidium atoms sometimes caused the atoms to spend a negative amount of time in their excited state. The clock, in some real physical sense, ran backward.

Here's the setup. When a photon hits an atom, it can be absorbed, boosting the atom into a higher energy state. The atom stays excited for some duration, then re-emits the photon and drops back down. This process has been understood since the early days of quantum mechanics. What Steinberg's team wanted to measure was precisely how long atoms stayed excited during photon transit through a medium — a seemingly straightforward question with a deeply strange answer.

They fired photons into a cloud of rubidium atoms cooled to near absolute zero (to minimize thermal noise) and measured the excitation duration with exquisite precision. For broadband photon pulses — those with a wide spread of frequencies — the results were normal. Atoms got excited, stayed excited for a predictable positive duration, and relaxed. But when the team used narrowband pulses (photons with a very specific frequency, tuned near the atomic resonance), the measured excitation times went negative. The atoms appeared to return to their ground state before the interaction should have ended.

The measured values were striking: for the most narrowband pulses, atoms showed an excitation time of (-0.82 +/- 0.31) times the natural timescale, while broadband pulses yielded a normal (0.54 +/- 0.28). The negative sign isn't a rounding error or instrument glitch — it was a consistent, reproducible result across many runs.

So what's actually happening? The key insight is that quantum mechanics doesn't work the way our everyday experience suggests. A photon passing through an atom cloud isn't like a ball bouncing off a wall with a definite before and after. Instead, the photon exists in a superposition of states — it is simultaneously absorbed-and-reemitted and not-absorbed-at-all. When you measure the system, you're collapsing a probability distribution, and that distribution can include temporal values that are, mathematically and physically, less than zero.

Steinberg's collaborator Josiah Sinclair offered an analogy: imagine a clock hand that sometimes ticks forward and sometimes ticks backward, with the final reading being the average of all possible paths. If the backward-ticking paths dominate under certain conditions (like narrowband excitation near resonance), the average reading is negative. The clock hand, on average, moved backward — not because time reversed, but because the quantum averaging of all possible histories yielded a negative mean.

"We don't want to say anything traveled backward in time," Steinberg was careful to clarify in interviews. "That's a misinterpretation." No information was transmitted faster than light. No signals went backward. Causality — the bedrock principle that causes precede effects — remains completely intact. What is broken is the intuition that physical durations must always be positive numbers. In the quantum regime, they don't have to be.

Not everyone is convinced the result is as profound as the headlines suggest. Theoretical physicist Sabine Hossenfelder published a widely viewed critique arguing that the "negative time" is better described as a phase shift — photons acquiring a phase change as they pass through the medium, which can be mathematically represented as a negative delay without requiring any revision to our understanding of time itself. This is a legitimate interpretation, and the debate over whether the result is genuinely novel physics or a repackaging of known optical phenomena remains active.

The paper, posted on arXiv in September 2024, was developed with a theoretical framework by Howard Wiseman of Griffith University in Australia. As of early 2026, it has not yet appeared in a peer-reviewed journal, though it has attracted enormous attention from both the physics community and the public.

Steinberg himself admits: "I don't currently have a path from what we've been looking at toward applications." The value of this work, for now, is conceptual. It forces physicists to confront the fact that even seemingly basic quantities — how long did this atom stay excited? — can have answers that violate common sense in the quantum domain. Time, at the smallest scales, is not the rigid, forward-marching arrow we experience. It's a probabilistic smear that can, under the right conditions, average out to something less than nothing.

Sources:

  • https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/evidence-of-negative-time-found-in-quantum-physics-experiment/
  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.03680
  • https://phys.org/news/2024-12-scientists-negative-quantum.html

item.title: Tottenham Sack Igor Tudor After Just Seven Games as Relegation Crisis Reaches Breaking Point
item.description:
Tottenham Hotspur have sacked Igor Tudor after just seven matches in charge, making him the third manager dismissed this season. The decision came during Sunday's humiliating 3-0 home defeat to Nottingham Forest -- reportedly finalized after Forest scored their third goal in what was supposed to be a must-win relegation six-pointer. Tudor leaves with a record of one win, one draw, and five losses, a disastrous stint that has pushed Spurs to 17th place, clinging to survival by a single point above the relegation zone.

Following up on yesterday's entry about Tottenham's unthinkable collapse, the situation has deteriorated even faster than anyone expected. Tudor was appointed to replace Thomas Frank, who himself replaced Ange Postecoglou earlier in the campaign. Three managers in one season is extraordinary for any Premier League club; for a side that finished in the Champions League places as recently as 2023, it borders on institutional meltdown. Tudor's assistant Bruno Saltor conducted the post-match press conference on Sunday, citing Tudor's "personal and family matters" for his absence, but the real message was clear before anyone reached the microphone.

Danny Murphy, the former Spurs midfielder turned pundit, said it was "impossible" for Tudor to continue, describing the atmosphere inside the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium as "deflated and toxic." Jamie Carragher was even harsher on Sky Sports, calling the appointment the "worst in Tottenham's history" and singling out Cristian Romero for a "stupid" admission about the squad's fragile mentality. The Argentine defender reportedly acknowledged that players' confidence evaporates the moment things go wrong -- a damning insight into a dressing room in freefall.

The managerial rumor mill is already spinning. Harry Redknapp, the 79-year-old former Spurs boss who led the club to the Champions League quarter-finals in 2011, has reportedly offered to return as temporary manager until the end of the season. It would be an extraordinary homecoming -- sentimental, slightly chaotic, and very Tottenham. That said, Redknapp had earlier this month appeared to rule out a return, suggesting the situation would need to be dire for him to consider it. It is, by any measure, dire. The club is also exploring managers with specific experience in Premier League relegation battles, a sentence that would have seemed absurd twelve months ago.

The raw numbers tell the story. Tottenham have not won a Premier League match since December 28, 2025 -- a winless run of 13 games. James Maddison and Dejan Kulusevski, the creative heartbeat of the team when fit, have missed the majority of the season through injury. The squad that Daniel Levy assembled for over $1 billion feels hollow and disjointed. Relegation probability models now give Spurs a 16.1% chance of going down, up from single digits just weeks ago.

Seven matches remain, starting with a trip to Sunderland -- another club scrapping near the bottom. That fixture, which would once have been an unremarkable mid-table affair, is now the most consequential match in Spurs' modern history. Whoever takes charge needs to stabilize a squad that looks broken in every sense. The clock is ticking, and Premier League survival for one of English football's most storied clubs is genuinely in doubt.

Sources:

  • https://www.football365.com/news/tottenham-decide-sack-tudor-offer-redknapp-temporary-role
  • https://www.nbcsports.com/soccer/news/igor-tudor-reaction-what-did-spurs-boss-say-after-losing-must-win-relegation-showdown-vs-forest
  • https://www.skysports.com/football/news/11095/13516063/tottenham-fighting-relegation-igor-tudor-has-made-a-disaster-start-to-spurs-reign-so-where-do-the-club-turn-next
  • https://www.teamtalk.com/tottenham-hotspur/igor-tudor-sack-next-tottenham-manager-harry-redknapp-return-sources

item.title: 五角大楼点名密歇根大学接棒常春藤，成为军官研修新合作院校——但联邦调查还在进行中
item.description:
2 月 27 日，国防部长 Pete Hegseth 签署备忘录，终止了五角大楼与全部五所常春藤盟校以及乔治城大学、塔夫茨大学的高级军事学院研修合作（Senior Service College Fellowship），转而点名密歇根大学、佛罗里达大学和内布拉斯加大学作为新的候选合作院校。

这个 SSC Fellowship 是什么？简单说，这是一个为期 10 个月的领导力培养项目，为有志晋升高级职位的军官提供国家安全与政策教育。以前这些名额几乎被常春藤垄断，哈佛肯尼迪学院是最大赢家。Hegseth 在视频声明中的措辞相当辛辣：这些学校"贪婪地吞噬美国纳税人的信任基金，却变成了反美情绪和军事蔑视的工厂。"

密歇根大学的反应很快。发言人 Kay Jarvis 宣布学校将为此前已被哈佛肯尼迪学院录取的军方申请者建立"快速审核通道"，减少"不必要的官僚障碍"。换句话说，密歇根正在积极争取把常春藤流失的军官学员接过来。

但整件事最耐人寻味的地方在于：密歇根大学目前同时面临至少两项联邦调查。第一项是教育部针对校园反犹主义的 Title VI 调查——2025 年 3 月，教育部以 60 所大学未能保护犹太学生免受骚扰为由展开调查，密歇根在名单上。第二项是教育部法律总顾问办公室发起的外国资金调查，原因是密歇根大学提交的外国资金报告存在"不准确和不完整的披露"，矛头直指中国方面的资金。

一边被调查、一边被选中当合作伙伴，这种矛盾局面折射出特朗普政府对高等教育的复杂态度：它在意识形态层面对精英私校开刀，但在实用层面又需要像密歇根这样有规模、有科研实力的公立大学来填补空缺。对密歇根大学而言，抓住这个机会既能展示"我们跟常春藤不一样"的姿态，也可能在联邦经费全面收紧的大背景下争取到一些政治筹码。

该合作预计从 2026-2027 学年开始生效。

Sources:

  • https://www.michigandaily.com/news/news-briefs/pentagon-taps-umich-as-potential-fellowship-partner-after-ending-ivy-league-programs/
  • https://www.detroitnews.com/story/news/politics/2026/03/17/umich-department-of-war-defense-partner-federal-investigations/89071349007/
  • https://www.wilx.com/2026/03/19/defense-department-eyes-university-michigan-hillsdale-college-military-education-programs/

item.title: 热刺0-3惨败森林后，图都尔下课进入倒计时：国际比赛日成最佳窗口
item.description:
上回我们报道了图都尔（Igor Tudor）执掌热刺三战全败的困境。情况没有好转——周日在主场，热刺被诺丁汉森林0-3碾压，Igor Jesus、Morgan Gibbs-White 和 Taiwo Awoniyi 各入一球。61,519名球迷在白鹿巷目睹了这场溃败。

这场比赛前，多家英媒报道称这是图都尔的"生死战"。结果揭晓后，Football Insider 率先透露：热刺高层已在安排紧急会议，CEO Vinai Venkatesham 将主导评估，Lewis 家族也将参与决策。据多个消息源称，图都尔"极不可能"在下一场对桑德兰的比赛中继续执教。

数据层面：图都尔二月上任以来，英超七场仅拿1分（0胜1平6负），热刺是英超唯一一支2026年至今联赛零胜的球队。唯一的亮点是欧冠16强3-2逆转马竞——但联赛才是生存命脉。热刺目前第17位，仅比降级区高1分，而俱乐部背负着7.725亿英镑的球场贷款。降级意味着年收入从约1.28亿英镑电视分成骤降至4895万的降落伞合同，对财务结构是毁灭性打击。

Jamie Carragher 赛后直言图都尔是"热刺历史上最差的任命"，呼吁立即换帅。接替人选方面，博彩公司开出的赔率显示：Roberto De Zerbi（4/1）被视为"梦想人选"，但他是否愿意在降级危机中接手存疑，更可能是夏窗到位；Mauricio Pochettino（3/1）始终在传闻中心，但他正备战世界杯的美国队帅位；Robbie Keane（3/1）执教费伦茨瓦罗斯表现出色，被认为最愿意立刻接手。短期救火方案中，Ryan Mason（10/1）和 Harry Redknapp 的名字也被提及。

国际比赛日为热刺提供了两周缓冲期——这几乎肯定是换帅的最佳窗口。如果热刺等到比赛日结束再行动，新帅将有至少一周时间熟悉球队后再迎战桑德兰。从去年11月波切蒂诺离任、Thomas Frank 短暂执教到图都尔的灾难性表现，这已经是热刺本赛季第三次教练危机。六年六任主帅，从欧冠决赛到濒临降级——白鹿巷的故事荒诞到让人不敢相信。

Sources:

  • https://www.sportbible.com/football/premier-league/tottenham-igor-tudor-sack-decision-097405-20260322
  • https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/48250030/tottenham-europa-league-relegation-igor-tudor-champions-leaguepremier-league-mark-ogden
  • https://www.teamtalk.com/tottenham-hotspur/vinai-venkatesham-igor-tudor-tottenham-sack-news-meeting-scheduled
  • https://www.givemesport.com/tottenham-next-manager-odds-football-soccer/

item.title: Defending Champions Liverpool Sit Fifth With 10 League Defeats as Injury-Ravaged Title Defense Collapses
item.description:
Liverpool's title defense has gone from underwhelming to outright alarming. The defending champions slipped to a 2-1 defeat at Brighton on March 21, their 10th league loss of the season -- the most they have suffered in a single Premier League campaign since 2016. Danny Welbeck scored twice for the Seagulls, the second a well-worked header in the 56th minute after Yankuba Minteh's cross was nodded back by Jack Hinshelwood. Milos Kerkez had briefly equalized for Liverpool in the 30th minute, capitalizing on a Lewis Dunk error, but it was never enough.

Liverpool now sit fifth in the table with 49 points from 31 matches, two points behind fourth-placed Aston Villa and a staggering 21 points behind leaders Arsenal. That gap tells you everything about where the title race stands -- it isn't one, at least not where Liverpool is concerned. The more pressing question is whether Arne Slot's side can even secure a Champions League place for next season, and right now that is far from guaranteed. Chelsea are just one point behind in sixth, Brentford and Everton lurk on 46 points in seventh and eighth, and the gap between fifth and eighth is just three points. One bad week and Liverpool could find themselves outside European qualification entirely.

Injuries have been the recurring villain. Mohamed Salah was already sidelined before the Brighton match, and first-choice goalkeeper Alisson has been in and out of the team all season, forcing Giorgi Mamardashvili into extended duty. Hugo Ekitike, who has been one of Liverpool's more effective attacking options this term, lasted just five minutes at Brighton before a collision with James Milner forced him off. Slot's post-match comments were weary rather than angry: "It has happened so many times to us this season and again now." The fatalism of a manager who has run out of answers to a problem beyond his control.

This is a far cry from the team that cruised to the Premier League title last season. Liverpool's 2024-25 campaign, Slot's first in charge after succeeding Jurgen Klopp, was a masterclass in consistency -- they barely put a foot wrong from September to May. The expectation was that Slot would build on that foundation. Instead, the squad has unraveled. The lack of significant summer reinforcements has been widely criticized, and the injury crisis has exposed a lack of depth that was masked by good health last season.

Brighton's fourth win in five games, meanwhile, lifted them into the top half and underlined how competitive the middle of the Premier League has become. For Liverpool, the international break offers a moment to regroup before the run-in, but Slot needs bodies back. The Champions League quarter-final against PSG looms on April 8, and Liverpool cannot afford to limp into that tie in their current state. Their league form -- three matches without a win heading into the break -- suggests a team running on fumes.

For context, Liverpool are still alive in the Champions League, where they beat Galatasaray in the Round of 16. But the domestic picture is bleak. Ten defeats in 31 games is relegation-adjacent form, not title-winning form. The question is no longer whether Liverpool can defend their crown -- that ship sailed months ago. It is whether they can salvage a top-four finish from the wreckage.

Sources:

  • https://www.skysports.com/football/news/11669/13521412/brighton-2-1-liverpool-hugo-ekitike-injured-as-danny-welbeck-inflicts-10th-defeat-on-reds-to-dent-champions-league-hopes
  • https://www.nbcsports.com/soccer/news/premier-league-2025-26-table-teams-standings-for-the-new-season
  • https://www.espn.com/soccer/report/_/gameId/740898

item.title: 王牌还是弃子？Alexandr Wang 权力重组风波背后的三方博弈
item.description:
3 月初的一系列报道让 Alexandr Wang 在 Meta 的处境看起来相当微妙。先是多家媒体援引消息称 Meta 把原本归 MSL 管辖的工程团队、数据管线和模型监督职责拆分到了其他高管名下——包括 Avocado 和 Mango 两个核心模型项目。然后 Maher Saba 从 Reality Labs 调任组建新的 Applied AI Engineering 部门，直接向 CTO Andrew Bosworth 汇报，不经过 Wang。这些动作叠加在一起，很容易读出一个结论：Wang 被架空了。

Meta 发言人 Andy Stone 在 X 上的回应很直接："Totally false...Wang helped create the new team, still runs MSL and TBD, has growing not waning influence. This is all so silly." 据后续报道，最初的"边缘化"叙事起源于一家印度媒体的误读，被其他报道循环引用后放大。

但官方否认不等于没有真实的张力。要理解这场权力重组，需要回到 2025 年秋天的一次关键战略会议。Wang 的立场是先把基础模型做到能跟 OpenAI、Google 正面竞争的水平，哪怕短期产品集成会慢一些。Chris Cox（首席产品官）和 Bosworth 的态度恰好相反：用 Facebook 和 Instagram 的海量数据优先训练能快速变现的模型，广告推荐和内容排序才是 Meta 的差异化优势。两个方向都有道理，但资源是有限的。

这个分歧的结果就是现在的"双轨结构"。Wang 继续管 MSL，做超级智能的长线研究和 Avocado/Mango 模型开发。Saba 的 Applied AI Engineering 部门则负责把模型能力快速落地到产品中——两个团队分别做工具接口和数据评估，采用 1:50 的极度扁平管理结构。官方口径是"紧密协作"，但汇报线是分开的：一边向 Zuckerberg（Wang），一边向 Bosworth（Saba）。

2025 年 12 月《金融时报》报道 Wang 曾对同事抱怨 Zuckerberg 对 AI 项目的"紧密掌控"限制了创造力、拖慢了进度。考虑到 Wang 加入 Meta 的代价是 143 亿美元（收购 Scale AI 49% 股权），Zuckerberg 盯得紧一点不难理解。而 LeCun 在 2025 年 11 月宁愿辞职也不愿向 Wang 汇报，说明 MSL 内部的人际关系也远不像发布会上那么和谐。

目前的格局是：Wang 名义上权力未减，但实际运营中多了一个跟他平行的工程部门来分担"模型到产品"的落地工作。如果 Avocado 在延期到 5 月后交出一份漂亮的成绩单，Wang 的地位会比现在稳得多。反过来，如果延期继续，Saba 团队用现有模型做出了不错的产品体验，那"先出产品、后追模型"的路线就会成为事实上的主线。结果取决于 Avocado 的最终表现。

Sources:

  • https://www.storyboard18.com/brand-makers/meta-reorganises-ai-teams-trims-oversight-of-highest-paid-employee-alexandr-wang-report-91327.htm
  • https://www.benzinga.com/markets/tech/26/03/51148272/meta-shuts-down-rumors-mark-zuckerberg-sidelined-chief-ai-officer-alexandr-wang-spokesperson-calls-report-false-this-is-so-silly
  • https://seekingalpha.com/news/4530225-metas-wang-reportedly-clashed-with-company-veterans-cox-bosworth-on-ai-goals
  • https://www.wionews.com/technology/meta-ai-chief-alexandr-wang-s-role-evolves-as-mark-zuckerberg-restructures-ai-leadership-1773040904464

item.title: The Animal That Threw Away Its Ability to Breathe — and Thrived
item.description:
Every animal on Earth breathes. It's one of the most fundamental rules of complex life: from blue whales to fruit flies, all animals use oxygen to power their cells through aerobic respiration, a process that happens inside mitochondria — the organelles that convert nutrients into ATP, the universal energy currency. This was considered a defining feature of animal life. Until researchers at Tel Aviv University proved it wrong.

In 2020, Dorothee Huchon and her team published a study in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences showing that Henneguya salminicola, a tiny parasite that infects salmon, has completely lost its mitochondrial genome. Not reduced it, not simplified it — eliminated it entirely. The organism has no mitochondrial DNA, no genes for aerobic respiration, and no capacity to use oxygen whatsoever. It is the first and, six years later, still the only known multicellular animal that does not breathe.

H. salminicola is a myxozoan — a group of microscopic parasites that belong to the phylum Cnidaria, making them distant relatives of jellyfish, corals, and sea anemones. At about eight millimeters, the parasite forms small whitish cysts in the muscles of salmon species across the Pacific, from Oregon to Alaska to Japan. Fishers call the condition "tapioca disease" or "milky flesh" because infected fillets develop visible white spots. It's not harmful to humans, just unappetizing — and salmon processors have been dealing with it for decades without knowing the creature inside had rewritten one of biology's most basic rules.

The discovery came from a straightforward but thorough approach. Huchon's team used deep sequencing to look for mitochondrial DNA in the parasite and found none. They then searched the nuclear genome for all the genes typically involved in mitochondrial replication, transcription, and function. Nearly all of them were gone. The organism still has structures that look like mitochondria under an electron microscope — small, double-membraned organelles called mitochondria-related organelles (MROs) — but they lack the genetic machinery to perform oxidative phosphorylation, the core chemical process of aerobic respiration.

So how does it survive? The honest answer is: nobody is entirely sure. The leading hypothesis is that H. salminicola steals ATP directly from its host's cells. Parasites that live embedded in host tissue are bathed in a nutrient-rich environment, and some researchers suggest the organism may absorb ready-made energy molecules through its cell membranes, bypassing the need to produce its own. Others point to anaerobic metabolic pathways — fermentation-like processes that don't require oxygen — as a possible supplementary energy source. But the specific mechanism hasn't been confirmed experimentally.

What makes this discovery so striking is the evolutionary trajectory it represents. H. salminicola didn't start out oxygen-free. Its ancestors were cnidarians with full mitochondrial genomes and normal aerobic metabolism. Over millions of years of parasitic life inside oxygen-poor muscle tissue, the organism gradually shed everything it didn't need. This is a well-documented evolutionary pattern called reductive evolution — parasites tend to lose complexity over time because their host provides what they'd otherwise need to build themselves. Tapeworms lost their digestive systems. Parasitic plants lost chlorophyll. But losing the ability to breathe entirely? That was unprecedented in the animal kingdom.

The finding also challenges how we define "animal." Aerobic respiration was considered one of the hallmarks separating multicellular animals from certain single-celled organisms like Giardia that manage fine without oxygen. Some protists (single-celled eukaryotes) have independently lost their mitochondria multiple times, and scientists assumed this was only possible for simple, small organisms. H. salminicola proved that assumption wrong: it's a multicellular creature, with differentiated cell types and even stinging cells (nematocysts, inherited from its cnidarian ancestors), and it has completely abandoned aerobic life.

There's an astrobiology angle too. One of the assumptions in the search for extraterrestrial life is that complex organisms require oxygen — it's one reason scientists get excited about detecting oxygen in exoplanet atmospheres. H. salminicola demonstrates that this assumption has at least one exception on our own planet. If evolution can produce an oxygen-free animal here, the theoretical space for what complex life might look like elsewhere just got wider.

Six years after publication, H. salminicola remains a lonely outlier. No other multicellular animal has been found to lack aerobic respiration. Whether this is because it's genuinely rare or because nobody has thought to check other obligate parasites is an open question. The myxozoans alone comprise over 2,000 described species, most of them poorly studied. Huchon's team has suggested that other myxozoans living in similarly anaerobic microenvironments may have undergone parallel reductions. The first animal that doesn't breathe might not be the last — we just haven't found the others yet.

Sources:

  • https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.1909907117
  • https://www.livescience.com/first-non-breathing-animal.html
  • https://astrobiology.com/2025/01/henneguya-salminicola-lacks-a-mitochondrial-genome-and-does-not-require-oxygen.html
  • https://www.sciencenews.org/article/butt-blowfly-larvae-mimic-termite-faces

item.title: 第 64 届安娜堡电影节明天开幕：6 天 89 部片，今年特设乌克兰电影单元
item.description:
北美最古老的先锋与实验电影节——安娜堡电影节（Ann Arbor Film Festival）——明天（3 月 24 日）在 Michigan Theater 拉开帷幕，一直持续到 3 月 29 日。错过现场的话，线上放映从 3 月 30 日延续到 4 月 13 日。

今年是第 64 届。从大约 2800 部投稿中，策展团队精选了 89 部影片，覆盖实验片、动画、纪录片、剧情片和表演影像，来自 20 多个国家。节目编排包含 12 个短片竞赛单元和 11 部长片。

开幕夜 6:30 PM 先在 Michigan Theater 大厅搞一场派对（往年到场 350-400 人），本地商家会设摊位；8:15 PM 正式开始第一场短片竞赛放映。

今年最值得关注的特别单元是乌克兰电影。电影节设置了一个周六的短片专场（6 部乌克兰短片）、两部周日长片，以及一个 VR 装置——观众可以通过虚拟现实体验乌克兰的真实景观。在战争持续的背景下，这个单元的策划显然带有鲜明的政治与人文关怀。

另一个特别单元庆祝电影节 LGBTQ 专题放映 25 周年。执行总监 Leslie Raymond 把这个传统视为电影节的身份标识之一。

票价方面：单场 9-15 美元，也有全场通票和周末通票可选。学生场（周三至周四下午 1 点）和评审场（周三至周四下午 3 点、周五下午 1 点）免费开放。每场放映结束后都有导演 Q&A，这大概是小型电影节最吸引人的地方——你能直接跟创作者聊。

安娜堡电影节一直是这座大学城文化身份的重要组成部分。它不追求商业大片，专注于实验性和先锋性，这种气质和安娜堡本身的调性很搭。住在附近的话，花一个晚上去 Michigan Theater 看一场完全出乎意料的短片集，是三月底最值得尝试的事之一。具体排片表和购票信息在 aafilmfest.org。

Sources:

  • https://www.wemu.org/wemu-news/2026-03-23/ready-to-go-the-64th-ann-arbor-film-festival
  • https://www.aafilmfest.org/64aaff
  • https://www.annarbor.org/blog/stories/post/film-fest/

item.title: 国际米兰四轮不胜，领先优势缩至6分：Serie A 争冠格局正在改写
item.description:
国际米兰正在以最糟糕的方式迎来国际比赛日。周日客场对阵保级队佛罗伦萨，Pio Esposito 开场不到一分钟头球破门，看似要轻松拿下。但 Cher Ndour 下半场补射扳平，最终1-1收场。这已经是国米各项赛事连续第四场不胜。

放在一个月前，这个结果不算什么。但如今榜单的变化让人紧张：AC米兰周六3-2击败都灵，升至第二名（63分，少赛一场）；那不勒斯在周五1-0赢下卡利亚里后拿到四连胜，以62分紧随其后。国米的领先优势从一月份的两位数急剧缩水——现在只剩6分，而赛季还有9轮要踢。

更让国米球迷不安的是佛罗伦萨这场暴露出的问题。Esposito 的闪电进球让球队进入"管理比赛"模式，但后防在下半场明显松懈，Ndour 的进球来自一次本可以解围的二次进攻。美联社报道指出，国米在最近四场比赛中场控制力严重下滑，Nicolò Barella 的状态波动尤为明显。

接下来的赛程对国米并不友好：客场拉齐奥、主场罗马都是硬仗。而 AC米兰在两周前德比战3-1击败国米后士气正盛，他们的追赶势头非常真实。主帅 Fonseca 的球队防守数据排联赛前三，攻防两端都在最佳状态。

这让我想起2023-24赛季的翻版——那年国米也在下半程经历了一段低迷期，只是当时领先优势更大，最终安全上岸。这一次6分的缓冲够不够？如果米兰和那不勒斯继续这个势头，答案可能在四月中旬就会揭晓。Serie A 的冠军悬念，在所有人以为尘埃落定的时候，又活了过来。

Sources:

  • https://www.washingtonpost.com/sports/soccer/2026/03/22/fiorentina-inter-como-serie-a/56220e42-2600-11f1-a0f2-3ba4c9fe08ac_story.html
  • https://abcnews.com/Sports/wireStory/inters-serie-lead-6-points-after-1-1-131304594
  • https://www.outlookindia.com/sports/football/serie-a-2025-26-matchday-31-sunday-roundup-inter-milan-fiorentina-como

item.title: Chelsea Lose Fourth Straight as Everton's Beto Brace Exposes a Team in Freefall
item.description:
Chelsea's season lurched further into crisis on March 21 as they were dismantled 3-0 by Everton at the Hill Dickinson Stadium, extending their losing streak to four consecutive Premier League matches. Beto scored twice -- a clinical finish in the 33rd minute and a second that slipped through Robert Sanchez's hands in the 62nd -- before Iliman Ndiaye added a stunning third in the 76th minute to cap a dominant Everton performance. The Toffees broke their new stadium's attendance record with 52,547 supporters witnessing a result that lifted them to seventh, just two points behind Chelsea in sixth.

For Liam Rosenior, Chelsea's head coach, the pressure is mounting fast. Four consecutive defeats represent the club's worst run of form this season, and his post-match assessment was notably strained. "The most disappointing evening so far," Rosenior called it, before insisting there was no lack of effort or belief in the squad. But effort without results is a finite currency in the Premier League, and Rosenior -- who took charge after Enzo Maresca's departure -- is learning that Chelsea's notoriously trigger-happy ownership group does not reward patience.

Chelsea currently sit sixth with 48 points, one point behind Liverpool in fifth and just two ahead of the Everton side that humbled them. The Champions League qualification battle -- the minimum expectation for a club that has spent over two billion dollars on transfers in the Todd Boehly era -- is slipping away from them in real time. They could have climbed to fourth with a win at Goodison Park; instead they are looking over their shoulder at a resurgent Everton and a Brentford side level on points in eighth. The margins are razor-thin, and Chelsea are on the wrong side of every one of them.

The defensive issues are particularly alarming. Sanchez's error for Beto's second goal was emblematic of a team that lacks composure under pressure. Chelsea have now conceded nine goals in their last four matches, a run that includes defeats to Arsenal, Aston Villa, and Manchester United before the Everton debacle. Jordan Pickford, by contrast, was outstanding for Everton, making several key saves to keep Chelsea at bay whenever they managed to muster an attack. The contrast in goalkeeping quality was stark.

Everton's resurgence under Sean Dyche has been one of the Premier League's quieter stories this season. The Toffees' move to their new 53,000-seat stadium on the Liverpool waterfront has coincided with a run of form that has taken them from mid-table anonymity to genuine European contention. Four wins in five matches, 46 points on the board, and a goal difference that keeps improving -- Dyche has done what Dyche does, grinding out results with a squad that nobody expected to compete at this level. Beto, the Portuguese striker who struggled at Everton initially, has hit form at exactly the right moment, and Ndiaye's emergence as a creative threat has given them another dimension.

Chelsea face the international break needing to find answers quickly. Their squad, bloated with expensive signings and still lacking cohesion, was supposed to challenge for the title by now. Instead, Rosenior is firefighting a top-four battle with a team that looks fragile mentally and defensively. The next few weeks will determine whether Chelsea's season ends with Champions League football or the Europa League -- and whether Rosenior is still in charge to see it through.

Sources:

  • https://www.skysports.com/football/news/11095/13521433/everton-3-0-chelsea-beto-brace-takes-toffees-seventh-as-pressure-builds-on-liam-rosenior-after-fourth-loss-in-a-row
  • https://www.espn.com/soccer/report/_/gameId/740899
  • https://www.chelseafc.com/en/news/article/rosenior-staying-focused-on-the-bigger-picture-and-an-international-break

item.title: RAPO（KDD'26）：用检索打破 Agentic RL 的探索困境——离线经验注入让训练又快又好（arXiv 2603.03078）
item.description:
当前 Agentic RL 的训练有一个根本瓶颈：纯 on-policy 生成意味着 agent 只能从自己的推理轨迹中学习。一旦陷入某种推理惯性，探索空间就被锁死了。打个不太恰当的比方：让一个厨师只靠反复尝试自己的菜来提高手艺，而从不允许看别人的菜谱——进步的天花板来得很快。

上海交大张思伟等人的 RAPO（Retrieval-Augmented Policy Optimization） 提出了一个直觉很清晰的解法：在训练 rollout 过程中，把检索到的离线推理经验注入 agent 的推理流，物理上打开探索边界。

核心机制分两层。第一层是 Hybrid-policy Agentic Rollout。系统维护一个 Step-Trace Buffer，存储来自更强模型的步骤级推理痕迹（注意是 step-level，不是整条 trajectory）。rollout 时，agent 每走一步都以 50% 的概率去 buffer 里检索一条语义最相似的离线 trace，替代自生成的推理步骤。这等于在 agent 自己摸索的过程中，随时递给它一张"别人在类似关卡的通关提示"。检索完成后 agent 继续独立推理——离线经验只是拓宽视野，不是代替思考。步骤级注入的好处是粒度细：agent 可以在任意一步获得新视角，而不需要被迫跟随一整条外部轨迹。

第二层是 Retrieval-aware Policy Optimization。混入离线 token 后，标准策略梯度会出问题——离线步骤的梯度贡献不受当前策略控制。RAPO 用两个机制校准。检索奖励（Retrieval Reward）通过熵减来量化每次检索的信息增益：如果检索后 agent 的输出不确定性显著下降，说明这条经验真的有用；同时引入时机信号，优先奖励在高不确定性节点上的检索行为。重要性塑形（Importance Shaping）根据每条轨迹中检索 token 的占比来重新加权策略梯度，并对检索导入的 token 做 mask 处理，避免梯度稀疏导致训练不稳定。最终训练目标整合了检索优势和任务结果奖励，配合类似 GRPO 的 token 级重要性采样和 KL 正则化。

实验覆盖 14 个数据集、三类 agentic 推理任务（包括多步工具调用、网页导航等），RAPO 平均提升 5.0%，训练效率提高 1.2 倍。两个数字放在一起很关键：不光学得更好，还学得更快——因为离线经验直接减少了 on-policy 生成的需求量，每个 rollout 的"信息密度"更高了。

从技术脉络看，RAPO 与此前跟踪的 RL for LLM 主线（GRPO、RewardFlow、Complementary RL 等）攻克的是不同层面的问题。后者改进的是"怎么算奖励""怎么更新策略"，RAPO 直接动的是更上游的瓶颈——训练时 agent 到底能看到什么经验。本质上这是 RAG 思想在 RL 训练循环中的一次有趣移植：推理时检索已经成为标配，训练时为什么不检索？当然局限也明显：Step-Trace Buffer 的质量和多样性取决于那个"更强的离线 agent"，冷启动阶段怎么办、buffer 如何持续更新，论文没有深入讨论。但方向本身很有想象力——未来的 Agentic RL 训练可能会标配一个经验库，就像人类学习时既做题也看解析。

Sources:

  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.03078

item.title: Titans and MIRAS: Google's Blueprint for AI Systems That Learn to Remember
item.description:
Most sequence models treat memory as an afterthought — a fixed-size vector or a sliding attention window that forgets everything beyond its horizon. Google Research has published two companion papers that take a fundamentally different approach: Titans, an architecture that uses a deep neural network as a persistent, learnable memory module, and MIRAS, a theoretical framework that unifies all modern sequence models under a single lens of associative memory. Together, they represent the most serious attempt yet to give neural networks something resembling genuine long-term memory.

The core idea behind Titans is deceptively simple. Instead of compressing history into a fixed-size recurrent state (like an LSTM or Mamba), Titans uses a multi-layer perceptron (MLP) as its long-term memory. This MLP learns to store and retrieve information through its own parameters, updated at test time as new tokens arrive. The key mechanism is a "surprise" metric: when incoming data diverges sharply from what the model has already memorized — a high-gradient signal — that information gets prioritized for storage. Low-surprise inputs (predictable continuations) can be processed without committing to permanent memory. This is not a metaphor — the gradient magnitude literally serves as the gating signal for memory writes.

Two additional mechanisms make this practical for very long sequences. First, a momentum system considers both momentary surprise and recent surprise context, so information that is individually unsurprising but contextually relevant still gets captured. Think of a sentence where each word is predictable but the overall meaning is new — momentum catches that. Second, an adaptive weight decay acts as a forgetting gate, discarding stale information when memory capacity fills up. This trio — surprise-based storage, momentum, and adaptive forgetting — gives Titans a psychologically plausible memory system that bears more resemblance to human memory consolidation than anything in the standard Transformer toolkit.

The paper describes three architectural variants for integrating the memory module. Memory as Context (MAC) feeds retrieved memories as additional context tokens into the attention mechanism and works best for very long sequences. Memory as Gate (MAG) uses retrieved information to modulate layer outputs, acting as a learned bias. Memory as Layer (MAL) inserts the memory module as a distinct processing stage between standard layers. In practice, MAC emerged as the strongest performer for long-context tasks, likely because it preserves the full expressiveness of attention while augmenting it with arbitrarily old retrieved information.

The results are striking. On the BABILong benchmark for extreme long-context reasoning, a 760M-parameter Titans model outperformed GPT-4 and Llama-3.1-70B despite having orders of magnitude fewer parameters. Titans scaled effectively beyond 2 million tokens while maintaining retrieval accuracy above 95% at 16,000-token needle-in-haystack tests. On standard language modeling benchmarks (C4, WikiText), Titans consistently beat Transformer++, Mamba-2, and Gated DeltaNet baselines of comparable size. Ablation studies confirmed that deeper memory modules (more MLP layers) consistently achieve lower perplexity, and — critically — performance holds as sequence length increases, a property standard Transformers lack due to their quadratic attention cost. The architecture also demonstrated effectiveness beyond language: genomic sequence modeling and time-series forecasting both showed gains over baselines.

MIRAS provides the theoretical scaffolding that makes Titans more than just another architecture paper. It decomposes any sequence model into four design choices: memory architecture (vector, matrix, or deep MLP), attentional bias (scoring rules for what to store), retention gate (information decay over time), and memory algorithm (the optimization process). Through this lens, Transformers, RNNs, SSMs like Mamba, and Titans itself are all variations of the same underlying concept: associative memory systems that differ in their resolution of these four design questions. This is a genuine contribution to architectural theory — it provides a principled way to navigate the design space rather than relying on architectural intuition.

Google used MIRAS to derive three new attention-free architectures: YAAD (using Huber loss for robustness against outlier tokens), MONETA (generalized norm penalties for stricter mathematical guarantees), and MEMORA (constraining memory as a probability map for stability). All three sometimes outperform Mamba-2 and standard Transformers in long-context evaluations. The real value is not any single architecture but the demonstration that principled design-space exploration can systematically produce competitive models.

For the broader field, this work represents a concrete step toward models that continuously learn from their input stream rather than relying solely on what was baked in during pretraining. The Titans architecture sidesteps the quadratic cost of full attention while preserving — and in some cases exceeding — the precision of Transformer-style retrieval. The open question is scalability: all experiments top out at 760M parameters. If surprise-based memory and test-time learning hold up at the 7B-70B scale where production models live, this could fundamentally change how we think about context windows — not as a fixed budget to be engineered around, but as an expanding resource that the model manages autonomously. Google has not yet released follow-up results at larger scales, so that remains the key thing to watch.

Sources:

  • https://research.google/blog/titans-miras-helping-ai-have-long-term-memory/
  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.00663
  • https://the-decoder.com/google-outlines-miras-and-titans-a-possible-path-toward-continuously-learning-ai/

item.title: 周四8场世界杯生死战：意大利第三次倒在附加赛？欧洲区最后4张门票争夺战前瞻
item.description:
距离2026世界杯欧洲区附加赛半决赛只剩三天。3月26日（周四），8场单场淘汰赛将同时打响（除土耳其 vs 罗马尼亚提前至北京时间凌晨1点），其余7场均在北京时间凌晨3:45开球。胜者进入3月31日的决赛，最终4支球队拿到加拿大/墨西哥/美国世界杯的最后门票。

A路：意大利的噩梦能终结吗？意大利在贝尔加莫主场迎战北爱尔兰。听起来轻松，但别忘了：这支意大利已经连续缺席了2018和2022两届世界杯——2018年被瑞典淘汰，2022年在附加赛半决赛被北马其顿绝杀。主帅 Gennaro Gattuso 接手后带来了一些改变，但挪威在小组赛中把意大利挤出了直通名额，这本身就说明问题。同路另一场是威尔士主场对波黑，Craig Bellamy 的球队在卡迪夫有不错的主场战绩，但威尔士在四次交手中从未赢过波黑（2平2负）。

B路：乌克兰 vs 瑞典的伤病困扰。瑞典新帅 Graham Potter 面临尴尬局面：头号射手 Alexander Isak 和 Dejan Kulusevski 均因伤缺阵。好消息是阿森纳的 Viktor Gyökeres 可用。乌克兰主帅 Rebrov 强调"谁状态最好谁就赢"。另一场波兰主场对阿尔巴尼亚，莱万多夫斯基领衔的波兰在15次交手中10胜，主场优势巨大。

C路：土耳其的天赋溢出。这可能是最有看点的一路。土耳其在小组赛6场打入17球，Arda Güler、Kenan Yıldız、Hakan Çalhanoğlu 的中前场组合堪称附加赛最强阵容。对手罗马尼亚由80岁的传奇教练 Mircea Lucescu 执教——他曾在2017-19年执教土耳其。另一场斯洛伐克对科索沃，科索沃正在争取队史首次大赛参赛资格，近5场不败（3胜2平）。

D路：丹麦的心理考验。丹麦本应直接出线，但在最后阶段先平白俄罗斯、再负苏格兰，被拉进附加赛。面对北马其顿——新帅 Goce Sedloski 刚上任不久——丹麦纸面实力占优，但心态是最大的敌人。同路捷克主场对爱尔兰，捷克在主场三连胜爱尔兰，但爱尔兰在预选赛中赢过葡萄牙，新帅 Heimir Hallgrímsson 说"爱尔兰足球的未来掌握在球员手中"。

八场比赛，四条路线，每条路线只有一个名额。对意大利来说，第三次倒在附加赛将是灾难性的；对科索沃来说，这是创造历史的机会；对丹麦来说，这是一场不能输的心理战。三天后见分晓。

Sources:

  • https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/news/02a2-1feaa5e4d15e-1e628dfba029-1000--world-cup-2026-european-play-off-semi-finals-what-to-look-ou/
  • https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/48223034/2026-world-cup-european-playoffs-explained-how-works-teams-schedule-how-watch-uk-tv-channel-italy-wales-northern-ireland-republic
  • https://statsbet.org/news/world-cup-2026-playoffs-all-uefa-semi-finals-march-26-guide

item.title: HiPER：把 Agent 的「规划」和「执行」拆成两层分别训练，长程任务信用分配终于有了干净解（arXiv 2602.16165）
item.description:
用 RL 训练 LLM Agent 做长程任务时，有一个被反复提到但始终没被漂亮解决的问题：信用分配（Credit Assignment）。Agent 花了 20 步完成一个任务，最终只拿到一个成功/失败的信号——到底是第 3 步选对了子目标，还是第 15 步的某个操作挽救了全局？标准 RL 方法用 GAE（Generalized Advantage Estimation）沿时间轴反向传播优势估计，但当步数多、奖励稀疏时，这个信号基本被噪声淹没。

明尼苏达大学洪明义组的 HiPER（Hierarchical Plan-Execute RL） 给出了一个结构化的答案：既然 Agent 做长程任务本来就分"想清楚做什么"和"动手做"两个阶段，那训练时也应该把这两层分开处理。

架构上，HiPER 把 LLM 的每一步输出拆成三个结构化字段：<switch>（二元决策：继续当前子目标 / 切换新子目标）、<subgoal>（自然语言描述的中间目标）、<action>（与环境交互的具体操作）。高层规划器负责提出和切换子目标，低层执行器在固定子目标下生成连续动作。一个子目标可能跨越多个执行步骤——比如在 ALFWorld 里，"去厨房找到刀"是一个子目标，下面对应"开门""走到厨房""打开抽屉""拿刀"四个动作。

关键创新在 Hierarchical Advantage Estimation（HAE）。低层的优势估计在每个子目标段内用标准 GAE 计算，但段的最后一步不是简单截断，而是 bootstrap 到高层的边界值——信用可以跨段传播。高层则把每个子目标段的累积奖励聚合成一个"宏步骤"，对子目标选择和切换决策做独立的 TD 残差计算。两层通过段边界耦合：低层知道"这个子目标段结束后全局进展如何"，高层知道"这个子目标段内部执行得怎样"。数学上可以证明 HAE 是无偏梯度估计器，且方差严格小于扁平 GAE。

实验用 Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct 在两个交互决策基准上测试。ALFWorld 上 HiPER 达到 97.4% 成功率，比之前最好的方法高 6.6 个百分点；WebShop 上 83.3%，高出 8.3 个百分点。更重要的数字是在长程子集上的增益——需要 5 个以上子目标的任务中，HiPER 相比扁平方法的优势从平均 6-8 个点拉大到 12-15 个点。这符合直觉：任务越长，层次化信用分配的优势越明显。

结合此前跟踪的 Agent 规划线索来看——ToolTree 用 MCTS 做工具选择搜索，StructuredAgent 用 AND/OR 树做任务分解，TraceR1 用 RL 训练预判能力——HiPER 走了第四条路：直接在 RL 训练目标里硬编码"规划≠执行"的结构。四种方法不是替代关系，而是可以组合的维度。一个有趣的开放问题是：HiPER 的分层策略能否和 ToolTree 的树搜索叠加——让高层用 MCTS 搜索子目标序列，低层在 HiPER 框架内执行？

Sources:

  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.16165

item.title: MIT's TLT: Turning Idle GPUs Into a 2x Speedup for Reasoning RL Training
item.description:
Reinforcement learning has become the dominant post-training method for building reasoning models — GRPO, DAPO, and RLVR have largely replaced classical RLHF as the alignment stack of choice. But RL training for reasoning models has a dirty secret: it is catastrophically inefficient. A team at MIT led by Song Han has published an updated version (v3, March 20, 2026) of their system called TLT (Taming the Long-Tail) that addresses the root cause and achieves 1.7-2.1x end-to-end speedups without any accuracy loss.

The problem is the long-tail distribution of response lengths during RL rollouts. When a reasoning model generates candidate answers for RL training, most responses are reasonably short — but a handful hit the maximum sequence length, consuming enormous GPU time while every other device sits idle waiting. The paper quantifies this precisely: rollout generation consumes up to 85% of total RL step execution time, and individual training steps can take around 40 minutes on 128 GPUs for a 32B-parameter model. The gap between the maximum configured sequence length and the 75th percentile of actual response lengths is enormous. In a typical batch, 90% of GPUs have finished their rollouts and are doing nothing while the remaining 10% grind through their maximum-length responses. This is the "long tail" that gives the paper its name.

TLT attacks this with two interlocking components. The first is an Adaptive Drafter Trainer. The insight is that during rollout generation, GPUs that finish shorter responses go idle. TLT puts those idle cycles to work training a lightweight draft model — a single transformer decoder layer — that learns to predict the larger model's outputs. This draft model trains continuously and asynchronously using cached hidden states from the target model's inference phase, so it stays synchronized with the evolving policy without consuming any additional compute budget. The training is decoupled from rollout completion: the drafter updates on partial or readily available data, overlapping with ongoing rollouts. A key engineering choice is selective asynchronous checkpointing, which the paper reports is 9.2x faster than vanilla checkpointing — essential when the drafter needs to update frequently without blocking the main training loop.

The second component is an Adaptive Rollout Engine that uses speculative decoding to accelerate the actual generation. The trained drafter proposes multiple tokens at once, the target model verifies them in a single forward pass, and accepted tokens skip the expensive autoregressive generation step entirely. What makes this "adaptive" is that the engine automatically selects optimal speculative decoding configurations — acceptance thresholds, draft sequence lengths, batch arrangements — based on the characteristics of each training batch. As the target model's policy evolves through RL, the drafter co-evolves with it, maintaining high acceptance rates throughout. The paper tracks this quantitatively: accept lengths improve from 4.59 to 6.53 tokens over the course of training, and the drafter sustains higher acceptance probabilities at distant token positions compared to vanilla (static) drafters. This co-evolution is what distinguishes TLT from simply bolting speculative decoding onto an RL pipeline.

The team evaluated TLT across four model architectures: Qwen2.5-7B, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B, Qwen2.5-32B, and Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct — covering a range from distilled reasoning models to large instruction-tuned systems. End-to-end speedups ranged from 1.7x to 2.1x over VeRL, the current state-of-the-art RL training framework, with zero degradation in downstream accuracy on any benchmark. For speculative decoding alone at batch size 1, speedups reached 3.2-3.6x depending on acceptance parameters. The approach scales well: at the 32B and 70B parameter scales where training costs are most painful, the speedups remained consistent.

There is an elegant bonus. The lightweight drafter model, trained as a byproduct of the RL process, is directly usable for efficient inference deployment afterward. You get a free speculative decoding draft model that is specifically tuned to your final reasoning policy — something that usually requires a separate distillation step with its own compute budget.

This work matters because RL-based reasoning training is becoming the default recipe for frontier model development. DeepSeek-R1, Qwen-QwQ, and the various "thinking" model families all rely on extensive RL rollouts, and the compute costs are staggering. A 2x speedup on the most expensive part of the pipeline — achieved without approximation or accuracy loss — has direct implications for the economics of training the next generation of reasoning models.

The paper also represents a broader trend worth tracking: the boundaries between training and inference are blurring. Speculative decoding was developed for serving; TLT migrates it into training. Test-time compute scaling was an inference concept; now it informs training curricula. The updated v3 release (March 20) adds experiments on 70B-parameter Llama and more detailed analysis of drafter acceptance dynamics. Code is available, making this immediately reproducible for any team running RL training at scale.

Sources:

  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.16665
  • https://news.mit.edu/2026/new-method-could-increase-llm-training-efficiency-0226
  • https://arxiv.org/html/2511.16665v1

item.title: 64th Ann Arbor Film Festival Opens Tomorrow: Ukrainian Cinema, VR, and 25 Years of LGBTQ Programming
item.description:
The world's oldest experimental and avant-garde film festival returns to the Michigan Theater this week. The 64th Ann Arbor Film Festival runs March 24-29, presenting 89 juried films selected from roughly 2,800 submissions across 12 shorts competition programs and 11 feature films. The opening night party kicks off Tuesday at 6:30 PM in the theater lobby — typically drawing 350-400 people among art installations, local vendors, and visiting filmmakers — followed by the first shorts competition screening at 8:15 PM. For a city that sometimes feels like it's only about football and tech startups, this is Ann Arbor at its cultural best.

This year's standout programming is a first-of-its-kind weekend devoted to Ukrainian filmmakers. The festival has always used its platform to amplify voices from conflict zones, and the Ukrainian focus arrives as the war grinds into its fourth year with diminishing international media coverage. Saturday at 3:30 PM, six contemporary Ukrainian shorts explore displacement, memory, ecological aftermath, and the psychological traces of conflict through poetic film essays, animation, documentary, and experimental fiction. The filmmakers approach these themes not as reportage but as art — exile as a lived condition, the fragility of home, intergenerational bonds severed and reformed. Sunday brings two Ukrainian feature films: Divia (directed by Dmytro Hreshko, a Netherlands/Poland/Ukraine co-production from 2025) screens at 12:30 PM, followed by Flowers of Ukraine (Adelina Borets, Poland, 2024) at 2:15 PM.

Perhaps the most visceral piece in the lineup is a room-scale VR installation called Is the War Close? by Joseph Andrew Sywenkyj. It places viewers inside a Kyiv apartment during a Russian drone and missile attack — not as passive observers but as participants occupying the space, looking around, hearing the walls shake. This is experiential filmmaking at its most direct, and it's running throughout the festival. The University of Michigan's Weiser Center for Europe and Eurasia covered submission fees for all Ukrainian films this season, a quiet institutional gesture that helped make this entire programming block possible. It's a good example of how UMich resources can directly enrich Ann Arbor's broader cultural calendar.

Sunday also marks the 25th anniversary of the festival's LGBTQ+ programming, which gets a dedicated retrospective block. That's a quarter-century of queer cinema at a festival that was championing these voices well before most institutions caught on — from the early 2000s, when featuring LGBTQ work was genuinely transgressive for a Midwest festival, to the present day when it's become a cornerstone of the program. The anniversary screening is worth attending for the historical arc alone.

Executive Director Leslie Raymond flagged another trend shaping this year's submissions: the quality leap in AI-generated filmmaking. The volume of AI submissions held steady from last year, Raymond said, but the quality is "remarkable" — filmmakers are moving past novelty experiments into genuinely expressive work with these tools. Local Michigan-based artists from Ann Arbor, Ypsilanti, Detroit, Grand Rapids, and Kalamazoo are well represented across formats including generative AI experiments, stop-motion animation, live cinema performance, and 360-degree VR. Featured local works include My Body Is a Lens I Can Look Through With My Mind and That Bolex Thing — titles that give you a fair idea of the festival's aesthetic wavelength.

Practical details for anyone planning to attend: free programs include student screenings Wednesday and Thursday at 1:00 PM and jurors' programs Wednesday through Friday afternoons — solid options if you want to sample the festival without spending a dime. Individual tickets for other programs run $9-15, with full festival and weekend passes available through aafilmfest.org. Two online programs will be available March 30 through April 13 for anyone who can't make it in person. But the real draw is being in the room. The filmmaker Q&As after most screenings are half the experience, and the conversations between programs in the Michigan Theater lobby — where the art installations transform the space into something between a gallery and a gathering — make opening night one of the best cultural evenings Ann Arbor offers all year.

Sources:

  • https://www.wemu.org/wemu-news/2026-03-23/ready-to-go-the-64th-ann-arbor-film-festival
  • https://www.aafilmfest.org/single-post/64-aaff-shines-a-light-on-ukrainian-filmmakers
  • https://www.ecurrent.com/film/a-quarter-of-a-century-of-lgbt-cinema-meets-improvements-with-ai-at-the-2026-ann-arbor-film-festival/
  • https://events.umich.edu/event/145475

item.title: HCAPO：让 LLM 自己当「事后诸葛亮」——后见之明信用分配把 ALFWorld 从 77.6% 拉到 91.4%（arXiv 2603.08754）
item.description:
长程 Agent 任务的稀疏奖励问题已经被反复讨论，但多数方案要么训练额外的过程奖励模型（Process Reward Model），要么依赖手工设计的中间信号。南京大学谭汇泽、李宇峰等人的 HCAPO 走了一条相当讨巧的路：既然 LLM 本身就擅长"给定答案后回头分析哪一步做对了"，为什么不直接让它当自己的事后评委？

Generative Verification 是这篇文章最核心的设计。做法其实不复杂：把成功轨迹的最终结果注入到 prompt 里，然后让 LLM 重新评估每一步动作的合理性。不需要训练额外模型，不需要外部标注——LLM 自己对着已知答案给每一步打分。具体产出是一个后见之明重要性比（hindsight importance ratio）ρ：对于那些在"已知成功"条件下概率更高的动作，ρ > 1（这步确实关键）；反之 ρ < 1（这步可有可无甚至在瞎忙）。ρ 经过 clip 和归一化处理后作为微观信号。

训练时，HCAPO 把两层信号合成一个复合优势函数：宏观层面用标准 GRPO 风格的轨迹级优势（成功轨迹高、失败轨迹低），微观层面用后见之明校正的步骤级 Q 值。公式是 A_HCAPO = A_GRPO + ω·(Q_H - μ_H)/σ_H，其中 ω 控制两层信号的混合比例。这种设计让宏观和微观信号互补：GRPO 负责"哪条路径更好"，后见之明负责"好在哪一步"。

实验结果相当扎实。在 ALFWorld（7B 模型）上，HCAPO 达到 91.4% 成功率，对比 GRPO 的 77.6% 提升了 13.8 个百分点，几乎追平 GiGPO 的 90.8%。WebShop 上成功率 73.8%（GRPO 66.1%，+7.7 个点），Score 85.1 vs 79.3。Search-augmented QA 任务上平均 48.3%，同样领先所有基线。

几个细节值得注意。第一，计算开销很低——Generative Verification 只占总训练时间的 8.3%，因为只需要对成功轨迹做一次前向传递。第二，一个有趣的副作用是 Agent 学会了更简洁的策略：训练后平均轨迹长度从 ~7.8 步缩短到 ~5.8 步。直觉上很好理解——后见之明信号帮 Agent 识别出了"瞎忙"的步骤，策略优化自然趋向精简。第三，1.5B 到 7B 的 scaling 表现良好，小模型也能受益。

把 HCAPO 和同期的 HiPER（层次化规划-执行分离）、RAPO（检索增强探索）放在一起看，三者攻克的是 Agentic RL 训练链的不同环节：RAPO 解决 agent 看到的经验不够多样，HiPER 解决信用分配的结构化缺失，HCAPO 解决现有经验中的步骤级归因模糊。三种方法理论上完全可以叠加——用 RAPO 扩大探索池，用 HiPER 的层次结构划分信用边界，再用 HCAPO 在每个子目标段内做精细归因。能不能叠出超线性收益，是个值得实验的开放问题。

Sources:

  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.08754

item.title: 利物浦伤兵满营进入国际比赛日：萨拉赫缺席埃及队，Isak 瞄准4月8日欧冠复出
item.description:
上周我们报道了利物浦1-2负于布莱顿的比赛，以及萨拉赫和 Ekitike 接连受伤的消息。经过几天的评估，伤情更新如下——有好消息，也有令人担忧的部分。

先说好消息：Hugo Ekitike 的伤势并不严重。他在布莱顿比赛开场仅两分钟就因与 James Milner 碰撞受伤离场，一度含泪退出让人心惊。但主帅 Arne Slot 赛后确认这只是死球伤（dead leg），"如果需要他明天就能上场"。Ekitike 已回到替补席，预计4月4日对曼城的比赛没有问题。

萨拉赫的情况就复杂得多。他在布莱顿比赛中主动要求换下，这对一个从不轻易认输的球员来说是个不好的信号。埃及足协确认萨拉赫将缺席3月31日对西班牙的比赛，这意味着伤势不是几天就能恢复的。利物浦方面没有公布具体伤情和时间表，但国际比赛日的两周休整期给了恢复空间。关键问题是：4月4日对曼城、4月8日欧冠八强首回合对 PSG——这两场比赛萨拉赫能不能赶上？

门将 Alisson Becker 的缺阵更让人揪心。他在社交媒体上表示自己要"缺席一段时间"，没有给出任何回归时间表。Alisson 本赛季已经多次因伤缺阵，稳定性是利物浦后防的一大隐患。远藤航（Wataru Endo）则在桑德兰足总杯比赛后接受了手术，目标是赛季末复出——他的时间线瞄准的是世界杯，联赛可能来不及了。

最值得期待的回归是 Alexander Isak。这位前纽卡前锋去年12月对热刺比赛中腿骨骨折，一月转会加盟利物浦后一直在养伤。他目前已经在草坪上单独训练，但还没有合练。俱乐部的目标是让他在4月8日欧冠对 PSG 的首回合复出——如果萨拉赫届时也能回归，利物浦的攻击线将首次迎来 Salah-Isak-Ekitike 的三叉戟组合，这也是一月砸重金引进 Isak 的核心构想。

国际比赛日对利物浦来说其实是及时雨。联赛10场失利、英超排名滑落到第五——Slot 需要这两周时间重新整合阵容，让伤员恢复，为4月上旬连续三场硬仗（曼城、PSG首回合、PSG次回合）做准备。布莱顿之败的苦涩，或许会在四月化为动力。

Sources:

  • https://www.liverpool.com/liverpool-fc-news/features/liverpool-injury-return-dates-ekitike-33637957
  • https://uk.sports.yahoo.com/news/liverpool-injury-hugo-ekitike-mohamed-070006395.html
  • https://www.nbcsports.com/soccer/news/hugo-ekitike-injury-news-latest-update-as-liverpool-star-forced-off-vs-brighton

item.title: Hugging Face's Spring 2026 Report: China Now Leads Open-Source AI Downloads, Robotics Datasets Explode 23x
item.description:
Hugging Face published its State of Open Source Spring 2026 report on March 17, and the numbers tell a story of rapid structural change in who builds AI, who uses it, and what it is being used for. The headline findings: China has overtaken the United States as the largest source of model downloads, independent developers now account for more model distribution than industry labs, and robotics has become the fastest-growing category on the platform by an extraordinary margin.

The platform itself has grown to 13 million users, over 2 million public models, and more than 500,000 public datasets. But the more interesting story is in the distribution patterns. Model concentration is extreme: the top 200 most-downloaded models — just 0.01% of the total — account for 49.6% of all downloads. Meanwhile, roughly half of all models on the Hub have fewer than 200 total downloads. This is a classic power-law distribution, but the scale of the long tail is remarkable. Two million models exist; the vast majority sit effectively unused.

China's ascent is the report's most geopolitically significant finding. Chinese organizations now represent approximately 41% of all Hugging Face downloads, surpassing the United States in both monthly and cumulative download volumes. The catalyst was DeepSeek-R1's viral release in January 2025, which the report identifies as the inflection point that accelerated the Chinese ecosystem's pivot to open-source distribution. The downstream effects have been dramatic: Baidu went from zero public releases in 2024 to over 100 in 2025, while ByteDance and Tencent each increased their releases 8-9x. Alibaba's Qwen family now has over 113,000 derivative models — more than Google and Meta combined. DeepSeek-R1 has become the most-liked model on the Hub, dethroning Meta's Llama from its long-held position.

The shift in who builds and distributes models is equally striking. Industry's share of model development dropped from roughly 70% before 2022 to about 37% in 2025, while independent (unaffiliated) developers rose from 17% to 39% of downloads. At peak, independent developers accounted for over 50% of distribution activity. These are not hobbyists making toy models — they are quantizing, fine-tuning, adapting, and redistributing models from major labs, creating the practical ecosystem that makes frontier models usable. The report frames this as the "derivative economy": the value chain has shifted from raw model creation to adaptation and deployment.

Then there is robotics. In 2024, robotics datasets on Hugging Face numbered 1,145, ranking 44th among categories. By 2025, that number exploded to 26,991 — a 23x increase — making robotics the single largest dataset category on the platform, surpassing text generation (roughly 5,000 datasets) by a wide margin. The LeRobot GitHub repository nearly tripled its star count. New large-scale datasets like RoboMIND (107,000+ real-world robot trajectories across 479 tasks) are driving this growth. Hugging Face's acquisition of Pollen Robotics appears to have catalyzed community activity, but the underlying driver is broader: robotics is transitioning from a hardware-limited field to a data-limited one, and the open-source community is racing to close the data gap.

A subtler finding concerns model size preferences. The mean size of downloaded models grew from 827M parameters in 2023 to 20.8B in 2025 — a dramatic shift. But the median barely moved: 326M to 406M. The interpretation is that a relatively small number of users are pulling very large models (driving up the mean), while the majority of practical deployment still happens at the 1-9B parameter range. This aligns with what we covered yesterday regarding Qwen 3.5 Small: the efficiency frontier at the 9B scale is where most production value is being extracted, even as headlines focus on trillion-parameter behemoths.

Other notable data points: over 30% of Fortune 500 companies now maintain verified Hugging Face accounts. South Korea's National Sovereign AI Initiative has named five national champions (LG AI Research, SK Telecom, Naver Cloud, NC AI, and Upstage) to produce competitive domestic models. And AI-for-science applications — protein folding, molecular dynamics, drug discovery — are expanding rapidly, with dedicated science teams now standard at all frontier companies.

The overall picture is of an ecosystem that has matured past the "release a model and declare victory" phase into something more structurally complex. The locus of activity is multipolar (US, China, Europe, Korea), the mode of participation is derivative (adaptation over creation), and the frontier is expanding into domains (robotics, science) that were niche a year ago. For anyone tracking where open-source AI is heading, this report is the most data-rich snapshot available.

Sources:

  • https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026
  • https://programming.dev/post/47531647
  • https://aitoolly.com/ai-news/article/2026-03-18-state-of-open-source-on-hugging-face-spring-2026-report-released-by-hugging-face-blog

item.title: Michigan Basketball Draws Alabama in Sweet 16: Friday Night in Chicago
item.description:
The bracket has spoken, and Michigan's Sweet 16 opponent is set. No. 4 seed Alabama demolished Texas Tech 90-65 on Sunday night in Tampa to earn a matchup against the No. 1 overall seed Wolverines. The game tips off Friday, March 28 at 7:35 PM ET at the United Center in Chicago — about a four-hour drive from Ann Arbor, for anyone contemplating a road trip. The game airs on TBS and TruTV.

Following up on yesterday's report about Michigan's dominant run through the first two rounds: Dusty May's squad has looked like a machine so far. The Wolverines beat Howard 101-80 and Saint Louis 95-72, winning both games by 20+ points with an efficiency that bordered on clinical. Big Ten Player of the Year Yaxel Lendeborg's 25-point performance against Saint Louis was historic — he became the first Michigan player with 25+ points and zero turnovers in an NCAA Tournament game since Glen Rice in 1989, the year Michigan won the national championship. The Wolverines also set a program NCAA Tournament record with nine blocks against the Billikens, anchored by 7-foot-3 center Aday Mara, the Big Ten Defensive Player of the Year with 96 blocks on the season. Michigan outrebounded Saint Louis 42-27, a margin that reflects the size advantage this team brings against almost anyone.

Alabama presents the first genuine test. The Crimson Tide are making their fourth consecutive Sweet 16 — a school record — and coach Nate Oats has built a program that knows how to play in March. Against Texas Tech, the Tide put on a three-point shooting clinic: Latrell Wrightsell Jr. went 6-of-9 from deep for 24 points, and Alabama as a team drained 19 threes compared to Texas Tech's four. Houston Mallette came off the bench for 15 points and 8 rebounds. The 90-65 final was Alabama's largest margin of victory in NCAA Tournament history. When this team's shooting is on, they can bury anyone in a hurry.

The key matchup Friday night is Michigan's interior defense versus Alabama's perimeter barrage. The Wolverines rank among the top five nationally in blocks per game, and Mara's presence in the paint deters opponents from attacking the rim. Alabama will likely be forced to beat Michigan from outside — which, given Sunday's performance, they're perfectly capable of doing. But Michigan's rebounding advantage could be decisive. If the Wolverines control the glass and limit Alabama to one-shot possessions, the Tide's three-point variance becomes a liability rather than a weapon. Michigan averages 82.5 points per game; Alabama averages 83.1. This should be an up-tempo affair with real tournament drama.

Two factors could swing it. First, Alabama's path was smoothed by Texas Tech playing without star forward JT Toppin, who has been out since February with a torn ACL — they haven't yet faced a defense as long and athletic as Michigan's. Second, the Wolverines are still missing sophomore guard L.J. Cason, out for the season with his own ACL tear. Cason's absence thins an already guard-light rotation, and Alabama's backcourt speed will pressure Michigan's perimeter defenders in ways that Howard and Saint Louis simply couldn't.

Still, this is a team that set a program record with 33 wins, led by the conference's best player, best defender, and coach of the year. Michigan hasn't reached the Elite Eight since 2021 and hasn't won a national championship since 1989. A win Friday would set up a regional final against the winner of Houston vs. Gonzaga on Sunday, March 30 — still at the United Center, still driveable. Student ticket packages were reportedly selling out fast on Sunday night. Given the proximity and the stakes, expect a sizable maize contingent in Chicago.

Sources:

  • https://sports.yahoo.com/mens-college-basketball/article/alabama-rolls-into-another-sweet-16-with-blowout-win-over-texas-tech-setting-up-battle-with-no-1-michigan-041822547.html
